nodes.name: Every 30 minutes
nodes.notes: Trigger every 30 minutes. Polling cadence is conservative for a personal pipeline; matches instagram-poster scan rate.

nodes.name: Scan Immich for new candidates
nodes.notes: Calls instagram-poster /scan endpoint. Returns {"new_items": ["asset-uuid", ...]}. Authorization header is optional (internal cluster service); leave INSTAGRAM_POSTER_TOKEN blank if unused.

nodes.name: Split new_items array
nodes.notes: Fan out one candidate per execution branch so each photo gets its own approval message.

nodes.name: Loop one at a time
nodes.notes: Process one asset at a time so errors on a single asset don't fan out and spam Telegram.

nodes.name: Fetch Immich asset metadata
nodes.notes: Pull asset metadata from Immich for caption preview (filename, fileCreatedAt, EXIF location).

nodes.name: Build caption + asset id
nodes.notes: Compose the caption from Immich metadata. HTML parse_mode is used downstream.

parameters.jsonBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ chat_id: $env.TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID, photo: $env.PUBLIC_INSTAGRAM_POSTER_URL + '/image/' + $json.asset_id, caption: $json.caption, parse_mode: 'HTML', reply_markup: { inline_keyboard: [[ { text: 'Approve', callback_data: 'approve:' + $json.asset_id }, { text: 'Reject', callback_data: 'reject:' + $json.asset_id } ]] } }) }}

nodes.name: Telegram sendPhoto with buttons
nodes.notes: Telegram needs a public URL for the photo (it fetches the image from outside the cluster). callback_data uses the action:asset_id format consumed by instagram-approval.
